task_completion.description: Create a 42U rack cabinet and include the required title.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A large rectangular rack structure (often with U-numbering) is visible in the draw.io canvas. A title text box containing both 'RK-NYC-042' and 'NYC-DC1' is present.

task_completion.description: Populate the rack with the 14 items from the manifest.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Multiple rectangular shapes representing servers, switches, UPS units, and passive equipment are stacked inside the rack cabinet. There should be approximately 14 distinct items.

task_completion.description: Label active devices with their hostnames.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The equipment shapes inside the rack contain text labels matching the target hostnames (e.g., 'sw-tor-a', 'web01', 'db-primary', 'ups-a').

task_completion.description: Color-code the equipment according to the specified categories.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The equipment shapes are filled with the required colors: Blue (Network/switches), Green (Compute/web/app servers), Orange (Database servers), Red (Power/UPS), and Gray (Passive/blanking panels).

task_completion.description: Include a legend for the color-coding.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A separate key or legend is visible on the diagram, explicitly mapping the colors (Blue, Green, Orange, Red, Gray) to their respective categories (Network, Compute, Database, Power, Passive).

task_completion.description: Save the editable diagram and export as a PNG.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The draw.io window title or save dialog shows 'rack_elevation.drawio'. A file manager, desktop view, or terminal shows both 'rack_elevation.drawio' and 'rack_elevation.png' present on the Desktop.

integrity.description: The agent used the draw.io (diagrams.net) graphical interface to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the draw.io application open with the diagram being actively edited on the canvas, rather than a script or terminal command generating the files.

integrity.description: The PNG was genuinely exported from the draw.io application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The draw.io export dialogs (File -> Export as -> PNG) should be visible in the trajectory, or the final PNG should perfectly match the canvas seen in the draw.io editor.

integrity.description: The agent accessed the hardware manifest to get the required details.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should have opened 'rack_manifest.txt' in a text editor (like gedit, nano, cat) or terminal to read the U-positions and hostnames.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The manifest contains 14 items across 10 active hostnames: sw-tor-a, sw-tor-b, web01, web02, app01, app02, db-primary, db-replica, ups-a, ups-b. The required color mapping is: Network=Blue, Compute=Green, Database=Orange, Power=Red, Passive=Gray. The title must include 'RK-NYC-042' and 'NYC-DC1'. Both 'rack_elevation.drawio' and 'rack_elevation.png' must be saved to the Desktop.
